medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient is a woman with HTN, HFpEF (severe TR, moderate MR), afib on warfarin, dyslipidemia, anemia, blindness, and NIDDM. Presenting with a few weeks of progressive shortness of breath. CT scan concerning for pericardial effusion and unilateral pleural effusion. No chest pain, mild shortness of breath, well-appearing in the emergency department. Exam notable for diminished breath sounds at the bases bilaterally and diminished heart sounds.
medical_history.past_medical_history: HTN, HFpEF (severe TR, moderate MR), afib on warfarin, dyslipidemia, anemia, blindness, glaucoma, NIDDM.
medical_history.family_history: Mother had a heart condition of unclear etiology.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital Signs: 98.2 PO, BP 119/63, HR 68, RR 18, SpO2 95% on 2L NC. Alert, oriented, no acute distress. JVP elevated. Pitting edema to shins.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Cardiovascular: Shows an irregular rhythm around 70 and an apical systolic ejection murmur radiating to the left sternal border. Lungs: Crackles at bases, no wheezes.
